title: erodium cicutarium subsp. primulaceum
intro: Erodium cicutarium subsp. primulaceum, also known as the pink-stemmed storksbill, is a subspecies of the annual herb Erodium cicutarium. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Erodium cicutarium subsp. primulaceum include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, typically combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
